assertions.description: The analyzer correctly identifies ctrl-M as a 2-layer board (B.Cu, F.Cu) with components on both sides (21 front, 18 back), 10 through-hole and 18 SMD parts, 445 track segments, 52 vias, routing_complete=true with 0 unrouted nets. The 4 extra footprints (PCB count 39 vs schematic 35) are accounted for by REF** silkscreen markers and additional connector footprints not in the schematic component list.

INCORRECT: All 39 footprints in the PCB output have side=null, even though each footprint correctly has a 'layer' field ('F.Cu' or 'B.Cu'). The statistics correctly derive front_side=21 and back_side=18 from the layer data, but the per-footprint 'side' field is never populated. The same null-side bug appears in crimpdeq-pcb. Similarly, PCB zones have layer=null despite having copper fill on specific layers. This affects any consumer that reads per-footprint or per-zone side/layer fields.
